source.title: Allostatic Self-efficacy: A Metacognitive Theory of Dyshomeostasis-Induced Fatigue and Depression

confidence_rationale: Load-bearing for SD-048's framing of fatigue drift as a slow agent-independent body-state perturbation. Stephan et al. give an explicit Bayesian / hierarchical predictive-coding account in which chronic dyshomeostasis -- persistently enhanced surprise about bodily signals, equivalently low evidence for the brain's interoceptive generative model -- is exactly the substrate-level structure SD-048's fatigue source instantiates: a slow AR(1) drift that does not arise from current motor output. The metacognitive layer reading allostatic self-efficacy as belief-about-the-controller's-competence maps onto the comparator gate the SD doc invokes (whether the agent's interoceptive forward model can keep up). Source quality very high (Stephan, Friston-trained TNU group, Seth + Fleming + Petzschner coauthors). Mapping fidelity high for Source 3 (fatigue) and Level 2 framing; lower for Sources 1 and 2 individually, which the framework subsumes but does not differentiate.
failure_signatures: If SD-048's fatigue source produces no detectable change in the comparator's prediction error or in the precision (Bayesian uncertainty) on its outputs, that would weaken Stephan et al.'s claim that chronic dyshomeostasis is the relevant interoceptive signature -- and weaken SD-048's case that fatigue drift is doing the substrate work it advertises.

If REE's interoceptive comparator never tracks fatigue drift because the slow AR(1) timescale exceeds the comparator's effective horizon, the prediction-error signature predicted by Stephan et al. for chronic dyshomeostasis cannot be obtained on this substrate -- a substrate-ceiling pattern.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Interoception is the inversion of a generative model of viscerosensory inputs. Dyshomeostasis is chronically enhanced surprise about bodily signals, equivalent to low evidence for the brain's model of bodily states. Allostasis is a change in prior beliefs (setpoints) for homeostatic reflex arcs. A metacognitive layer monitors interoceptive-allostatic circuit performance and updates beliefs about the brain's capacity to regulate bodily states (allostatic self-efficacy). Fatigue is the early adaptive response to dyshomeostasis; depression is the chronic response when allostatic self-efficacy collapses (cf. learned helplessness).
mapping.ree_translation: SD-048's fatigue drift source is the substrate-level instantiation of Stephan et al.'s 'chronic dyshomeostasis' -- a slowly accumulating body-state cost that persists independently of what the agent is currently doing. The AR(1) latent fatigue state is a minimal generative source for the kind of chronic prediction-error signature the metacognitive theory predicts. ARC-058's HarmForwardTrunk and ARC-033's HarmForwardSeparate both inherit the Stephan framework's commitment that an interoceptive comparator must be observable; SD-048 is what makes that commitment honestly testable in V3 by ensuring the comparator has agent-independent variance to learn against. The 4-arm noise-level sweep in the SD doc maps onto the framework's prediction that dyshomeostasis intensity (and thus comparator load) should scale monotonically with interoceptive perturbation amplitude up to the regime where the comparator saturates.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Stephan et al. is a theoretical / clinical-translational paper, not a substrate-level mechanistic study. It does not specify the statistical character (Gaussian vs Poisson vs AR(1)) of the underlying body-state perturbations -- SD-048's three-source decomposition is informed by, but not derived from, this framework. The mapping from 'chronic dyshomeostasis' to AR(1) fatigue drift is a reasonable architectural choice but other generative shapes (e.g. heavy-tailed bursts) would also fit Stephan's account. The framework is silent on whether comparator competence should follow an inverted-U with noise amplitude (Asai-style); SD-048's ARM_2 prediction draws that from Asai 2016, not from Stephan.
mapping.source_context: Theoretical review article in Frontiers in Human Neuroscience, 2016. Synthesises predictive coding, allostasis, metacognition, and computational psychiatry literatures. Empirical anchor in multiple sclerosis fatigue and depression.

confidence_components.notes: Stephan + Seth + Petzschner authorship + Frontiers in Human Neuroscience venue. Mapping fidelity high because the framework explicitly predicts a fatigue-as-dyshomeostasis-signature, which is what SD-048 implements. Transfer risk low for the substrate framing, moderate for the specific AR(1) shape choice (the framework does not adjudicate between drift shapes).
